title: potentilla arenaria acclinis
intro: Potentilla arenaria acclinis, also known as the sandy cinquefoil, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family Rosaceae, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and creeping habit. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Potentilla arenaria acclinis include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular remedy for skin conditions and minor injuries. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
